case_1:
library(meta)
library(ggplot2)

# (1) Forest plot: 8 studija
make_forest <- function(data, out_path) {
  res <- metabin(event.e, n.e, event.c, n.c, data = data, sm = "OR")
  pdf(out_path, width = 8, height = 6, family = "Times New Roman", res = 300)
  forest(res)
  dev.off()
}

# (2) PRISMA 2020 flow
make_prisma_flow <- function(n_screened, n_included, out_path) {
  # PRISMA flow logic; export PDF
  pdf(out_path, width = 7, height = 9, family = "serif")
  # ... prisma flow diagram code ...
  dev.off()
}

ggsave("figure_1.pdf", dpi = 300, width = 8, height = 6)
case_2:
# CONSORT 2010 flow: screened 450, excluded 230 (ineligible 180, odbili 50), randomized 220.
# TIVA n=110 (izgubljeni iz pracenja: 5), Sevo n=110 (izgubljeni: 8). ITT: TIVA n=105, Sevo n=102.
library(DiagrammeR)
n_screened <- 450
n_randomized <- 220
# ... consort diagram nodes: 450 screened, 230 excluded, 220 randomized; lost to follow-up 5 and 8; ITT 105, 102.
case_3: S obzirom na I2=94% i samo 3 studije, heterogenost je visoka. Pooling u meta-analizi s toliko malo studija i tako visokom heterogenošcu je upitan; preporuka je upozorenje u interpretaciji. Ogranicenje: mali broj studija i visoka I2. Razmislite o subgroup analizi ako postoje prikladni moderatori, ili o narativnoj sintezi umjesto poolinga.
